title: From Fine-Tuning to Prompting
concept: Instead of fine-tuning model weights, LLMs are steered using natural language prompts, shifting the engineering focus to prompt design.

exercises.prompt: When we talk about 'model weights' in AI, what do we mean?

options.text: The physical weight of the computer running the AI
The internal mathematical parameters that determine the AI's behavior
The number of words in the prompt

exercises.explain: An LLM's weights are the internal mathematical connections that determine how it processes information. Fine-tuning adjusts these weights, while prompting leaves them frozen.

cards.title: No Brain Surgery Required
cards.body: Imagine if every time you wanted to learn a new game, you had to undergo brain surgery to rearrange your neurons! That is what fine-tuning is like for an AI. Luckily, there is a much easier way to teach AIs new tricks: just talk to them.

cards.title: Fine-Tuning vs. Prompting
cards.body: Historically, adapting an AI to a new task meant 'fine-tuning' it: changing its internal mathematical weights using lots of training data. Today, we often use 'prompting'—simply writing natural language instructions to guide the existing model without changing its weights.

cards.title: The Workshop vs. The Sticky Note
cards.body: Imagine a programmable robot. Fine-tuning is like taking the robot to a workshop, opening up its metal body, and rewiring its internal circuits to make it sweep the floor. Prompting is like leaving the circuits alone and simply handing the robot a sticky note that says: 'Please sweep the floor.'

cards.title: Prompting in Action
cards.body: If you want an AI to write polite emails, you don't need to rebuild it. You just start your prompt with: 'You are a polite customer service assistant. Rewrite this email to be more friendly.' The AI uses its existing knowledge to adapt instantly.

cards.title: Do We Always Need to Train?
cards.body: Some believe that to make an AI good at a specific task, you must always retrain its brain (fine-tune it). In reality, modern Large Language Models are so smart that a well-written prompt is often all you need to get amazing results instantly.

cards.title: Comparing the Two Approaches
cards.body: See how the engineering focus shifts when we move from modifying the AI's internal brain to guiding its behavior with text.

right.text: Weights remain frozen. Uses natural language instructions to guide behavior instantly.

left.text: Modifies internal weights. Requires training data, code, and compute power.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary difference between fine-tuning a model and prompting it?

options.text: Fine-tuning changes the model's internal weights; prompting does not.
Prompting is much slower and requires supercomputers.
Fine-tuning is done with simple English text inputs.
There is no difference; they are two names for the same thing.

exercises.explain: Fine-tuning changes the model's internal parameters (weights) through training, while prompting leaves the weights exactly as they are and guides the model using text input.

exercises.prompt: True or False: With prompting, you can use the exact same AI model to write code, translate languages, and write stories without altering its brain.
exercises.explain: Prompting allows us to reuse the same frozen model weights for entirely different tasks just by changing the input text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence about modern AI control.
exercises.sentence: Instead of changing the mathematical weights of a model, modern AI engineering focuses on steering it through ________.
exercises.explain: Instead of changing the model's weights through heavy training, prompting allows us to control the model using everyday language.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of steering an AI using a prompt?

options.text: "Explain quantum physics to a five-year-old using a baking analogy."

exercises.explain: The second prompt is written by a human to guide the AI, while the first and third are generated by code or machine processes.

exercises.prompt: If we don't change a model's internal weights, how do we steer it to translate a sentence or write a poem?

options.text: By rewriting the model's core source code from scratch
By retraining the model on millions of new books to change its weights
By giving it clear instructions and context in natural language
By physically plugging in a new memory drive to its hardware

exercises.explain: By writing a clear prompt, we guide the pre-trained model to perform the specific task we want without modifying a single internal weight. This makes it fast and flexible!
